name: Distill meeting notes with Google Sheets and Gmail and log the results

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05638-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Recent from Gmail

### Read Recent from Gmail

Polls Gmail for messages received since `={{ $now.minus({hours: 24}).toISO() }}` — the source step for an email-driven pipeline; switch to a Gmail Trigger for push instead of polling.

Next: Newest First

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Next: Render as HTML

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Render as HTML

Converts markdown to HTML for rich rendering in email/CMS.

Next: Post Digest to Gmail

### Post Digest to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Next: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Archive Digest in Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Archive Digest in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
# Distill meeting notes with Google Sheets and Gmail and log the results

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill meeting notes with Google Sheets and Gmail and log the results. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual social copy is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Recent from Gmail → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Render as HTML → Post Digest to Gmail → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating social copy in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Summarization uses a Chain, not an Agent, because the step is fixed — there's no decision about which tool to call, just 'summarize this text.' Posting to multiple channels runs as parallel branches, not sequential sends, so a slow API on one channel never delays the message that was already ready on another.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a third notification channel and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Change 'Trim to Digest Size' from 15 to 5 and see how it narrows the summary's scope.
3. Swap the Summarization Chain for a Q&A Chain to answer questions instead of just recapping.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your social copy process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
